channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: One Year After Launch, Peak Surpasses 20 Million Players
item.content:encoded:
Peak continues to be at the peak (pun intended) of the friendslop mountain.

With the popular co-op climbing game turning a year old, it continues to see thousands of players per day on Steam. But despite the game not seeing the concurrent player total it had at this time last year, that doesn’t mean it’s not still having success.

In fact, the game recently confirmed it surpassed a major milestone that not many games will ever reach.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news from Google Play or the App Store.

Peak Hits 20 Million Players

Speaking with Tucker Hazell of Dev Dive for a 1st Anniversary interview, developer Aggro Crab revealed that Peak has surpassed 20 million players since its release.

“I do think we should be approaching this as if Peak was our lightning-in-a-bottle moment as far as numbers go,” Aggro Crab co-founder Caelan Pollock said. “We’re trying not to let it raise the bar way too much as far as the marketability and the sellability of what we put out there. I would really not want to be sacrificing what we want to do creatively because we want every game from now on to potentially be that 20 million player hit [like Peak].”

  • How To Get Rich | Take-Two CEO Reveals The “Secrets”

In addition to Peak’s performance, Aggro Crab and Hazell talk about how much the game has changed over the year, the reception of the “daily map” structure, the BBNO$ in-game concert, and much more.

What do you think of Peak passing 20 million players? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about Xbox’s chief strategy officer shutting down rumors that the company was already reconsidering its return to exclusive games. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: T1 Name K-pop Star FIFTY FIFTY’s Athena as Valorant Ambassador
item.content:encoded:
T1 has announced that Athena, one of the idols in K-pop group FIFTY FIFTY, has become a Valorant ambassador for the esports organisation.

The South Korean organisation is looking to grow its audience, having also signed popular Tyson ‘TenZ’ Ngo as a content creator last month.

FIFTY FIFTY have around 6.9 million monthly listeners on Spotify, with their song ‘Cupid’ racking up over 1.1 billion plays.

Meanwhile, T1’s VCT Pacific team are having a mixed season, having attended Valorant Masters Santiago earlier in the year but then missed out on Masters London.

If you haven’t already, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android:

  • Android Link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.insidergaming.app
  • iOS Link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/insider-gaming/id6753984648

Athena’s Valorant Fandom

This isn’t a random celebrity endorsement. Athena is a very keen Valorant fan and a strong player too, as she peaked at Immortal 1, 97 RR.

Though Athena admitted in the ambassador announcement video that her rank has tanked because of her busy idol schedule.

According to the K-pop star, she usually plays smokes and has named Astra, Sage, and Vyse as her favorite Agents.

  • MORE: G2 and Team Heretics eliminated from Valorant Masters London

Athena had appeared in a Valorant video with T1 back in November in their VALORANT One-Day Class video.

Now, she has been named T1’s official ambassador and immediately shared her excitement after learning of her new role:

“It’s been about four to five years since I started playing Valorant. And since day one, I’ve always wanted to do something official with Valorant… And getting to do this with such an iconic team like T1 it literally feels like a dream come true. I’m so happy.”

What other industries would you like to see crossover with gaming and esports? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And check out every Valorant team qualified for the Esports World Cup taking place later this year.

item.title: Jynxzi Couldn’t Save LCS Spring 2026 From Record Low Viewership
item.content:encoded:
The League of Legends Championship Series (LCS) drew the North American league’s lowest-ever viewership in Spring 2026.

The LCS Spring 2026 regular season attracted a new low of 86k viewers as interest in the LoL league dropped.

Now, according to Esports Charts, the LCS Spring 2026 Playoffs only peaked at 183,152 live viewers, an all-time low for an LCS split.

The peak viewership came during the LCS Playoffs final as LYON swept Team Liquid in three straight games.

If you haven’t already, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android:

  • Android Link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.insidergaming.app
  • iOS Link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/insider-gaming/id6753984648

Jynxzi Fails to Have an Impact

And this was despite the fact that popular streamer Nicholas ‘Jynxzi’ Stewart co-streamed the final between LYON and Team Liquid.

Earlier this year, Jynxzi hosted a content creator LoL tournament with over 900k viewers, one of the most-watched LoL events this year.

In comparison, his co-stream of the LCS finals appears to have peaked at only 44,416.

Though, that comparison may be unfair considering the content creator event benefitted from various streamer’s audiences and the free Twitch Drops.

LCS Spring 2026 Flops

Surprisingly, the LCS Spring Split Playoffs’ peak audience of just over 183k was not NA’s biggest audience in 2026.

The LYON and Team Liquid final just barely failed to beat the LCS Lock-In, which peaked at 184,969 viewers during LYON vs Cloud9.

It seems the continued failures of Cloud9, one of the LCS’s most decorated a popular teams, is hurting the league.

Related News: Fnatic CEO confirms there have been acquisition bids but claims the brand “isn’t going anywhere.”

Before this year, no LCS split had failed to crack the 200,000 viewers milestone.

There was also an alarming drop off in average viewership this split.

LCS Spring averaged 63,949 viewers, while the Lock-In recorded 89,516 during the Lock-In, representing a drop of almost 29%.

What does the LCS need to do to make the league interesting again? Share your thoughts with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

In other LCS-adjacent news, Team Liquid face a stern test as the MSI 2026 Play-In Bracket has been revealed.
